title: Tuna Avocado Salad Recipe (320 Cal, 34g Protein) | Eati
introduction: Tuna Avocado Salad is a high-protein meal delivering 34g of protein and 320 calories per serving — designed for muscle building and fat loss. With 14g of fat and 10g of carbohydrates, it fits comfortably into any macro-tracked eating plan. Log it in Eati to track your macros automatically and stay on target.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- 160g canned tuna in water, drained
- 1/2 medium avocado (75g)
- 80g cherry tomatoes, halved
- 30g cucumber, diced
- 2 cups (60g) mixed greens or romaine
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- Salt, pepper, chili flakes
- Optional: 1 tsp olive oil or capers

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Drain tuna thoroughly, break into chunks.
2. Slice avocado into wedges or dice.
3. Combine greens, tuna, avocado, tomatoes, and cucumber in a bowl.
4. Dress with lemon juice, olive oil (if using), salt, pepper, chili flakes.
5. Toss gently to avoid breaking avocado.
6. Log in Eati: 'tuna canned 160g, avocado 75g, mixed salad'.

sections.heading: Calories, Protein, Fat & Carbs
sections.content:
Per serving (estimated):

Calories: 320 kcal
Protein: 34g
Fat: 14g
Carbohydrates: 10g

Macro split: 42% protein / 39% fat / 12% carbs

Protein per 100 calories: 10.6g

Note: values are estimates based on typical ingredient weights. Log exact portions in Eati for precise tracking — the app calculates macros automatically from your description or a barcode scan.

sections.heading: Why Tuna Avocado Salad Works for Muscle Gain and Weight Loss
sections.content:
Tuna avocado salad is a zero-cook, 5-minute meal that delivers 34g of protein with healthy fats from avocado — a combination that satisfies hunger quickly without requiring any heat or significant prep time.

Macro breakdown (estimated):
• 320 calories
• 34g protein
• 14g fat (mostly monounsaturated from avocado)
• 10g carbohydrates

Avocado's role in fat loss:
Avocado's monounsaturated fats slow gastric emptying, which means you stay full longer per calorie consumed. A 2019 study in Nutrients found that adding half an avocado to lunch reduced appetite by 40% in the subsequent 5 hours — a significant advantage during a calorie deficit.

sections.heading: Variations and Serving Ideas
sections.content:
1. With egg (380 cal, 40g protein): add 2 boiled eggs.
2. On toast (420 cal, 35g protein): serve on 1 slice whole-grain toast.
3. Mexican (335 cal, 34g protein): add corn, jalapeño, cumin, cilantro.

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Storage & Time-Saving Tips
sections.content: Mix salad without dressing (3-day refrigerated). Add avocado and dressing fresh each time (avocado browns within 2 hours).

midArticleCta: Track the exact macros for Tuna Avocado Salad in Eati — type what you ate or snap a photo and let the app calculate protein, calories, fat, and carbs instantly.
conclusion: Tuna Avocado Salad delivers 34g of protein in 320 calories — a macro profile that makes it one of the most effective dishes for body recomposition. Add it to your weekly rotation, track each serving in Eati, and let consistent protein intake do the work.
metaTitle: Tuna Avocado Salad Recipe: 320 Cal, 34g Protein, 14g Fat, 10g Carbs
metaDescription: Tuna Avocado Salad recipe for muscle gain and fat loss — 320 calories, 34g protein, 14g fat, 10g carbs per serving. With ingredients, instructions, and meal prep tips.

faqs.question: How many calories in tuna avocado salad?
faqs.answer: About 320 calories — 34g protein, 14g fat, 10g carbs — using 160g canned tuna and half an avocado.

faqs.question: Is tuna avocado salad good for fat loss?
faqs.answer: Excellent. The protein from tuna suppresses hunger and the avocado fat slows gastric emptying. Studies show half an avocado at lunch reduces appetite for up to 5 hours.
